behavior_in_task_description.reason: Instruction names the target file (strings.rs), function (percent_format_positional_count_mismatch), fixture path, snapshot path, and all test-case inputs/expected counts.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction describes WHAT to flag (non-tuple literals) and what not to flag (variables/attrs/subs/calls) without prescribing the ResolvedPythonType mechanism.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests run ruff binary and count F507 diagnostics on code snippets; no assertion on specific variable names or line positions in the implementation.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests invoke ruff as subprocess and parse diagnostics; the AST checks for constraints are supplementary, not the sole verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: Fail-to-pass tests require F507 to fire on specific non-tuple RHS while pass-to-pass requires 0 F507 on variables/calls — a stub cannot satisfy both.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All behavior examples from instruction.md (literals, unary/binary ops, variables, calls, single-placeholder, tuple mismatch) have corresponding tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with section comments (Gates, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass, Config-derived, Repo CI/CD) and descriptively named.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a Rust linter bug fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths, function names, and commands are consistent and correct.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: Instruction names file/function but not specific line numbers or the exact fix mechanism; localization within the function is still required.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: Code-only task, no agent config edits.
